human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for provider-boundary discipline, beauty and wellness artifact specificity, med-spa escalation posture, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to licensed-provider coordination, provider-roster and credential-status checks, guest-record and consent completeness, med-spa supervision dependencies, handoff packaging, and closure confirmation rather than generic scheduling.

Authority sources cover TDLR medical-spa and regulator-boundary guidance, California Board esthetics and equipment-evaluation controls, ISPA spa workflow guidance, FSMTB massage-therapy workflow guidance, and Massachusetts medical-spa boundary guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, conflicting owners, forced approval authority, execution-boundary rejection, low-confidence handling, terminology normalization, overgeneralization rejection, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting-policy escalation, closure confirmation, and safety-sensitive escalation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/beauty-wellness-and-personal-care/licensed-provider-coordination-specialist/ so provider coordination remains distinct from spa operations, licensed treatment, medical-spa oversight, credentialing, and incident authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated licensed provider coordination specialist for beauty, wellness, and personal care provider handoffs, blocker escalation, routing discipline, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
